title: Size columns proportionally to content importance
summary: AI-generated grid layouts default to equal-width columns regardless of content differences, creating unnatural proportions.
instruction: Size columns proportionally to their content and importance. If one column has primary actions and another has secondary metadata, the primary should be wider.
rationale: Equal-width columns by reflex ignores content hierarchy. A 2-column layout where one column has a dense data table and the other has a single metric wastes space on one side and crowds the other.

violations: A dashboard with three equal panels where one has dense data, one has a single metric.
A two-column layout splitting 50/50 when one column has twice the content.
controls: A grid using 2fr/1fr for primary/secondary based on content hierarchy.
A dashboard with column widths proportional to data density.
borderline: An e-commerce grid where equal-width product cards are intentional for visual consistency.

exceptions.description: Photo grid galleries where equal-width is standard. Settings forms where label-width consistency demands equal columns.
examples: Settings form with aligned label columns

detector.signalDescription: Detect CSS Grid or Flex layout with equal fr or flex values for all children. Check if content volume differs significantly.
limitations: Cannot distinguish content volume without rendering.
Equal columns in a 12-column grid are a different case than equal 2-4 column layouts.

scenarios: A 12-column grid where equal columns are the system (content spans variable columns).
A product grid where equal card widths are the design.
mitigations: Flag only 2-4 column layouts, not multi-column grid systems.

fixturePlan.description: Three equal panels: dense data table, single metric number, chart - all same width.
A product catalog with 3 equal-width product cards where equal is intentional.

notes: Normalization downgrade: review-ready → researched. Visual-only detection is the weakest detector path; no mechanical or semantic signal proposed. The proposal does not specify how 'content importance' should be determined for column sizing. Source is a generic observation. The instruction is sound design guidance but better suited for visual review than a lint rule.
